abstract: Abstract Digital mental health has become an established part of mental health care, but the rapid arrival of large language models and other artificial intelligence (AI) tools has refocused attention on the evidence needed to guide the field. This editorial updates the research priorities articulated by JMIR Mental Health in 2023, while reaffirming their emphasis on equity, replicability, privacy, efficacy, and engagement. While the importance of these priorities has not changed in recent years, the urgency with which they must now be applied has. As digital tools become more clinically consequential, research must move beyond demonstrating that a technology is feasible, usable, or novel. The field now needs studies that clarify how these tools work, for whom they are beneficial, under what conditions they may cause harm, and how they can be ethically integrated into care. We call for research that is transparent about the technologies being studied, grounded in meaningful clinical questions, attentive to safety, and designed to produce knowledge that remains useful as specific products and models change.

mh.ai_reason: AI/LLM时代数字心理健康研究优先事项的社论，AI是核心焦点，内容涉及AI工具整合心理健康护理的伦理、安全与证据需求。
mh.tldr: 该社论更新了数字心理健康研究优先事项，强调AI工具需超越可行性演示，转向阐明机制、受益人群、安全条件和伦理整合。

title: Digital Mental Health Research Priorities, Revisited for the AI and Large Language Model Era
title_zh: AI和大语言模型时代数字心理健康研究优先事项再审视
